value.0: High Ledgerkeeper Caelthir Vaelorian is an austere and reserved man who carries himself with a measured composure that leaves little room for idle conversation or frivolity. Though distant in manner, his presence reflects a disciplined and deliberate character shaped by long years of careful thought and restraint.
A copy of this item (including any crafting you do on it) is used by your Bottled Companion.
Many of these elves were called from Evermeet itself, serving as highly-trained guardians of Winya Ravana.
Hoods are lighter than helmets and come in a variety of colors.
Hoods are lighter than helmets and come in a variety of colors.
This is a settlement guard insignia for the guard templates.
Many of these elves were called from Evermeet itself, highly trained in the art of archery. With their exceptionally keen senses, they serve as the scouts of Winya Ravana.
Hoods are lighter than helmets and come in a variety of colors.
Hoods are lighter than helmets and come in a variety of colors.
This is a settlement guard insignia for the guard templates.

These creatures have a level of intellect comparable to most dogs, but they have very independent spirits and are generally distrustful of creatures that aren't elves or half-elves.

Cooshee are quite effective fighters. When engaging someone in combat, cooshee often try to knock their opponent down with their large forepaws before biting them. These bites are bone-crushing, capable of felling an orc warrior. If ordered to subdue an opponent, cooshee simply close their jaws over their target's throat, resting their teeth against its skin. The threat is always clear.
Hoods are lighter than helmets and come in a variety of colors.
Hoods are lighter than helmets and come in a variety of colors.
This is a settlement guard insignia for the guard templates.

Value.value: *sniffs*

value.0: A resident of Winya Ravana's Inner Sanctum, carrying themselves with quiet dignity and the air of one raised among the city's elven traditions.

Value.value: The Council will decide. It always does, eventually.
The guard patrols less here. We require them less.
We sweep the Inner Sanctum's streets each morning. It is simply done.
If you intend to live here, learn the quiet hours.
Mind the roots beneath the paving.
You are welcome here, so long as you remain respectful.
Our forest has stood this long; it will stand longer.
It is customary to greet your neighbours.
Greetings of the Fair Folk to you.
Be mindful around the Lifestone Memorial. It deserves no less.
Warm winds and sweet laughter.
Alae.
What is it?
Blessings of the Seldarine upon you.
You can ask your questions to Xilow over the bridge.
I remember when this street had more trees.
Find a space within the noise, and listen to the silence. You'll be surprised what wisdom you can hear.
The seasons change, but the city remains.
We value peace, here - not quiet. Outsiders oft confuse the two.
Be at peace here, and leave strife outside where it belongs.

value.0: A resident of Winya Ravana's Outer Sanctum, at ease among travellers and traders and familiar with the bustle of the city's Outer Sanctum.

Value.value: The forest grows best when shared with friends.
It's good to see a friendly face in the street.
The breeze feels nice today, doesn't it?
I always enjoy seeing neighbours on this path.
I remember when this street had more trees.
I love seeing flowers in bloom along this path.
It feels like the city itself is smiling today.
I've learned to smile politely when the halflings argue over food.
We elves are patient. Humans? Less so.
Cordor? Cetha? Southport? These humans need to decide on a name.
I don't know how Barak Runedar endures so much cold.
At least it's only the gnolls we have to worry about now in the north.
Another halfling was reprimanded by our defenders for trying to cross the bridge.
You should visit the Moonlit Springs and unwind if you need to relax.
What is it?
Alae!
If you need to sell anything, the Long and Short in the Crafting Hall is the best place to go.
Outer Sanctum handles the city's trade, while the Inner handles its memory.
The first settlement on the island? You're standing in it.
Some elves never cross the bridge. I have, many times.
